claim: The 4D framework (Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence) applies universally across automation, augmentation, and agency interaction modes with AI
actionable: Use the 4D framework as a consistent evaluation model regardless of which AI interaction pattern you're using
source_title: Lesson 2B: The 4D Framework | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Description involves specifying what you need, format of output, how AI should approach the task, context/information needed, and tone/style of interaction
actionable: Create a description checklist covering output format, approach, context, and interaction style before each AI delegation
source_title: Lesson 2B: The 4D Framework | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Most AI interactions involve small loops of description and discernment: describing what you need, evaluating what you get, refining your request, and repeating
actionable: Design AI workflows as iterative loops where description and discernment feedback reinforce each other
source_title: Lesson 2B: The 4D Framework | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: Diligence requires considering fairness and bias in AI-assisted hiring, verifying accuracy of important decisions, protecting sensitive data, and being transparent about AI involvement
actionable: Implement audit processes for AI-assisted decisions in sensitive domains like hiring, credit decisions, and policy enforcement
source_title: Lesson 2B: The 4D Framework | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course

claim: The 4D competencies are not tied to specific tools or techniques but are fundamental skills that help adapt and grow alongside evolving technology
actionable: Invest in 4D competencies as timeless skills rather than tool-specific knowledge that may become outdated
source_title: Lesson 2B: The 4D Framework | AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations Course
